## nbinomWaldTest (function)

**Location:** R/core.R:1333
**Signature:** `(object,
                           betaPrior=FALSE, betaPriorVar,
                           modelMatrix=NULL, modelMatrixType,
                           betaTol=1e-8, maxit=100, useOptim=TRUE, quiet=FALSE,
                           useT=FALSE, df, useQR=TRUE, minmu=0.5)`

```r
1333	nbinomWaldTest <- function(object,
1334	                           betaPrior=FALSE, betaPriorVar,
1335	                           modelMatrix=NULL, modelMatrixType,
1336	                           betaTol=1e-8, maxit=100, useOptim=TRUE, quiet=FALSE,
1337	                           useT=FALSE, df, useQR=TRUE, minmu=0.5) {
1338	  if (is.null(dispersions(object))) {
1339	    stop("testing requires dispersion estimates, first call estimateDispersions()")
1340	  }
1341	  stopifnot(length(maxit)==1)
1342	  # in case the class of the mcols(mcols(object)) are not character
1343	  object <- sanitizeRowRanges(object)
1344	
1345	  # integer check
1346	  if ( !is.integer( counts(object) ) ) {
1347	    stop( "the count data is not in integer mode and fitType is not 'glmGamPoi'" )
1348	  }
1349	  
1350	  if ("results" %in% mcols(mcols(object))$type) {
1351	    if (!quiet) message("found results columns, replacing these")
1352	    object <- removeResults(object)
1353	  }
1354	  if (is.null(mcols(object)$allZero)) {
1355	    object <- getBaseMeansAndVariances(object)
1356	  }
1357	  
1358	  # only continue on the rows with non-zero row mean
1359	  objectNZ <- object[!mcols(object)$allZero,,drop=FALSE]
1360	
1361	  # model matrix not provided...
1362	  if (is.null(modelMatrix)) {
1363	    modelAsFormula <- TRUE
1364	    termsOrder <- attr(terms.formula(design(object)),"order")
1365	    interactionPresent <- any(termsOrder > 1)
1366	    if (missing(betaPrior)) {
1367	      betaPrior <- FALSE
1368	    }
1369	
1370	    # run some tests common to DESeq, nbinomWaldTest, nbinomLRT
1371	    designAndArgChecker(object, betaPrior)
1372	
1373	    # what kind of model matrix to use
1374	    stopifnot(is.logical(betaPrior))
1375	    blindDesign <- design(object) == formula(~ 1)
1376	    if (blindDesign) {
1377	      betaPrior <- FALSE
1378	    }
1379	    if (missing(modelMatrixType) || is.null(modelMatrixType)) {
1380	      modelMatrixType <- if (betaPrior) {
1381	        "expanded"
1382	      } else {
1383	        "standard"
1384	      }
1385	    }
1386	    if (modelMatrixType == "expanded" & !betaPrior) {
1387	      stop("expanded model matrices require a beta prior")
1388	    }
1389	    # store modelMatrixType so it can be accessed by estimateBetaPriorVar
1390	    attr(object, "modelMatrixType") <- modelMatrixType
1391	    hasIntercept <- attr(terms(design(object)),"intercept") == 1
1392	    renameCols <- hasIntercept
1393	  } else {
1394	    # modelMatrix is not NULL, user-supplied
1395	    if (missing(betaPrior)) {
1396	      betaPrior <- FALSE
1397	    }
1398	    if (betaPrior) {
1399	      if (missing(betaPriorVar)) stop("user-supplied model matrix with betaPrior=TRUE requires supplying betaPriorVar")
1400	    }
1401	    modelAsFormula <- FALSE
1402	    attr(object, "modelMatrixType") <- "user-supplied"
1403	    renameCols <- FALSE
1404	  }
1405	
1406	  if (!betaPrior) {
1407	    # fit the negative binomial GLM without a prior
1408	    # (in actuality a very wide prior with standard deviation 1e3 on log2 fold changes)
1409	    fit <- fitNbinomGLMs(objectNZ,
1410	                         betaTol=betaTol, maxit=maxit,
1411	                         useOptim=useOptim, useQR=useQR,
1412	                         renameCols=renameCols,
1413	                         modelMatrix=modelMatrix,
1414	                         minmu=minmu)
1415	    H <- fit$hat_diagonals
1416	    mu <- fit$mu
1417	    modelMatrix <- fit$modelMatrix
1418	    modelMatrixNames <- fit$modelMatrixNames
1419	    # record the wide prior variance which was used in fitting
1420	    betaPriorVar <- rep(1e6, ncol(fit$modelMatrix))
1421	  } else {
1422	    priorFitList <- fitGLMsWithPrior(object=object,
1423	                                     betaTol=betaTol, maxit=maxit,
1424	                                     useOptim=useOptim, useQR=useQR,
1425	                                     betaPriorVar=betaPriorVar,
1426	                                     modelMatrix=modelMatrix,
1427	                                     minmu=minmu)
1428	    fit <- priorFitList$fit
1429	    H <- priorFitList$H
1430	    mu <- priorFitList$mu
1431	    betaPriorVar <- priorFitList$betaPriorVar
1432	    modelMatrix <- priorFitList$modelMatrix
1433	    mleBetaMatrix <- priorFitList$mleBetaMatrix
1434	
1435	    # will add the MLE betas, so remove any which exist already
1436	    # (possibly coming from estimateMLEForBetaPriorVar)
1437	    mcols(object) <- mcols(object)[,grep("MLE_",names(mcols(object)),invert=TRUE)]
1438	  }
1439	
1440	  # store 'mu' and 'H', the hat matrix diagonals
1441	  dimnames(mu) <- NULL
1442	  assays(objectNZ, withDimnames=FALSE)[["mu"]] <- mu
1443	  assays(object, withDimnames=FALSE)[["mu"]] <- buildMatrixWithNARows(mu, mcols(object)$allZero)
1444	  dimnames(H) <- NULL
1445	  assays(objectNZ, withDimnames=FALSE)[["H"]] <- H
1446	  assays(object, withDimnames=FALSE)[["H"]] <- buildMatrixWithNARows(H, mcols(object)$allZero)
1447	  
1448	  # store the prior variance directly as an attribute
1449	  # of the DESeqDataSet object, so it can be pulled later by
1450	  # the results function (necessary for setting max Cook's distance)
1451	  attr(object,"betaPrior") <- betaPrior
1452	  attr(object,"betaPriorVar") <- betaPriorVar
1453	  attr(object,"modelMatrix") <- modelMatrix
1454	  attr(object,"test") <- "Wald"
1455	
1456	  # calculate Cook's distance
1457	  dispModelMatrix <- if (modelAsFormula) {
1458	    getModelMatrix(object)
1459	  } else {
1460	    modelMatrix
1461	  }
1462	  attr(object,"dispModelMatrix") <- dispModelMatrix
1463	  cooks <- calculateCooksDistance(objectNZ, H, dispModelMatrix)
1464	
1465	  # record maximum Cook's
1466	  maxCooks <- recordMaxCooks(design(object), colData(object), dispModelMatrix, cooks, nrow(objectNZ))
1467	
1468	  # store Cook's distance for each sample
1469	  assays(object, withDimnames=FALSE)[["cooks"]] <- buildMatrixWithNARows(cooks, mcols(object)$allZero)
1470	  
1471	  # add betas, standard errors and Wald p-values to the object
1472	  modelMatrixNames <- colnames(modelMatrix)
1473	  betaMatrix <- fit$betaMatrix
1474	  colnames(betaMatrix) <- modelMatrixNames
1475	  betaSE <- fit$betaSE
1476	  colnames(betaSE) <- paste0("SE_",modelMatrixNames)
1477	  WaldStatistic <- betaMatrix/betaSE
1478	  colnames(WaldStatistic) <- paste0("WaldStatistic_",modelMatrixNames)
1479	
1480	  #################################
1481	  ## t distribution for p-values ##
1482	  #################################
1483	  
1484	  if (useT) {
1485	    # if the `df` was provided to nbinomWaldTest...
1486	    if (!missing(df)) {
1487	      stopifnot(length(df) == 1 | length(df) == nrow(object))
1488	      if (length(df) == 1) {
1489	        df <- rep(df, nrow(objectNZ))
1490	      } else {
1491	        # the `WaldStatistic` vector is along nonzero rows of `object`
1492	        df <- df[!mcols(object)$allZero]
1493	      }
1494	    } else {
1495	      # df was missing, so compute it from the number of samples (w.r.t. weights)
1496	      # and the number of coefficients
1497	      if ("weights" %in% assayNames(object)) {
1498	        # this checks that weights are OK and normalizes to have rowMax == 1
1499	        # (although this has already happened earlier in estDispGeneEst and estDispMAP...
1500	        wlist <- getAndCheckWeights(objectNZ, dispModelMatrix)
1501	        num.samps <- MatrixGenerics::rowSums(wlist$weights)
1502	      } else {
1503	        num.samps <- rep(ncol(object), nrow(objectNZ))
1504	      }
1505	      df <- num.samps - ncol(dispModelMatrix)
1506	    }
1507	    df <- ifelse(df > 0, df, NA)
1508	    stopifnot(length(df) == nrow(WaldStatistic))
1509	    # use a t distribution to calculate the p-value
1510	    WaldPvalue <- 2*pt(abs(WaldStatistic),df=df,lower.tail=FALSE)
1511	  } else {
1512	    # the default DESeq2 p-value: use the standard Normal
1513	    WaldPvalue <- 2*pnorm(abs(WaldStatistic),lower.tail=FALSE)
1514	  }
1515	  colnames(WaldPvalue) <- paste0("WaldPvalue_",modelMatrixNames)
1516	  
1517	  betaConv <- fit$betaConv
1518	
1519	  if (any(!betaConv)) {
1520	    if (!quiet) message(paste(sum(!betaConv),"rows did not converge in beta, labelled in mcols(object)$betaConv. Use larger maxit argument with nbinomWaldTest"))
1521	  }
1522	
1523	  mleBetas <- if (betaPrior) {
1524	    matrixToList(mleBetaMatrix)
1525	  } else {
1526	    NULL
1527	  }
1528	
1529	  # if useT need to add the t degrees of freedom to the end of resultsList
1530	  tDFList <- if (useT) list(tDegreesFreedom=df) else NULL
1531	  
1532	  resultsList <- c(matrixToList(betaMatrix),
1533	                   matrixToList(betaSE),
1534	                   mleBetas,
1535	                   matrixToList(WaldStatistic),
1536	                   matrixToList(WaldPvalue),
1537	                   list(betaConv = betaConv,
1538	                        betaIter = fit$betaIter,
1539	                        deviance = -2 * fit$logLike,
1540	                        maxCooks = maxCooks),
1541	                   tDFList)
1542	  
1543	  WaldResults <- buildDataFrameWithNARows(resultsList, mcols(object)$allZero)
1544	  
1545	  modelMatrixNamesSpaces <- gsub("_"," ",modelMatrixNames)
1546	
1547	  lfcType <- if (attr(object,"betaPrior")) "MAP" else "MLE"
1548	  coefInfo <- paste(paste0("log2 fold change (",lfcType,"):"),modelMatrixNamesSpaces)
1549	  seInfo <- paste("standard error:",modelMatrixNamesSpaces)
1550	  mleInfo <- if (betaPrior) {
1551	    gsub("_"," ",colnames(mleBetaMatrix))
1552	  } else {
1553	    NULL
1554	  }
1555	  statInfo <- paste("Wald statistic:",modelMatrixNamesSpaces)
1556	  pvalInfo <- paste("Wald test p-value:",modelMatrixNamesSpaces)
1557	
1558	  tDFDescription <- if (useT) "t degrees of freedom for Wald test" else NULL  
1559	  mcolsWaldResults <- DataFrame(type = rep("results",ncol(WaldResults)),
1560	                                  description = c(coefInfo, seInfo, mleInfo, statInfo, pvalInfo,
1561	                                    "convergence of betas",
1562	                                    "iterations for betas",
1563	                                    "deviance for the fitted model",
1564	                                    "maximum Cook's distance for row",
1565	                                    tDFDescription))
1566	  
1567	  mcols(WaldResults) <- mcolsWaldResults
1568	 
1569	  mcols(object) <- cbind(mcols(object),WaldResults)
1570	  return(object)
1571	}
```
